entry.title: Re: Planned Delivery Cost of Purchasing in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi @Qi_Liu 

I have a question regarding custom purchasing condition types in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

I have followed the configuration steps to create a new planned delivery cost condition type ZCDT (Custom Duty):

  • Created condition type ZCDT by copying FVA1.
  • Added ZCDT to the purchasing calculation schema.
  • Assigned the updated calculation schema to all relevant schema groups.
  • Ensured the Manual – Manual Only indicator is maintained as recommended in KBA 3707393.

However, when I open the Change Purchasing Info Record (ME11) and try to add a new condition, ZCDT is not available in the list of condition types.

Could you please help clarify the following:

  1. Is it supported to have multiple custom planned delivery cost condition types (for example, Freight and Custom Duty) maintained in the Purchasing Info Record?
  2. If yes, is there any additional configuration or restriction that would prevent ZCDT from being available in the Purchasing Info Record?

Thank you very much for your help.

Wei

entry.title: Re: Item 002 Order XXXX budget exceeded, error message BP604
entry.content: Hi Raymond. Thank you for your reply. Checked everything but the double consumption was always occur. We wrote to SAP and the answer was given in SAP note 2674625. It requested to mention the cost element to make it exempted from the availability control. Controlling --> Internal Order --> Budgeting and Availability Control --> Specify Exempt Cost Elements from Availability Control.

entry.title: Re: Item 002 Order XXXX budget exceeded, error message BP604
entry.content:
Answer is given in SAP Note 2674625

If you want an account assignment of the investment measure in asset postings for the settlement of the investment measure, this also updates statistical actual values to the investment measure. However, you should then eliminate these statistical values from the calculation of the assigned values.

You can eliminate the statistical actual values from the assigned value of the CO availability control. To achieve this, you must define the balance sheet APC accounts of the affected assets under construction and of the affected completed assets as exempt cost elements of the CO availability control. To do this, call the following path in customizing (transaction SPRO):

Controlling => Internal Orders => Budgeting and Availability Control => Specify Exempt Cost Elements from Availability Control.

entry.title: Re: BusinessPartnerSUITEBulkReplicateRequest API throws e...
entry.content:
Hi Naga,

 

The error "BP already exists" most likely occurs when key mapping is missing for an existing BP in the source system (and possibly the target system as well), causing <ReceiverUUID> and <ReceiverInternalID> to be absent from the payload — as a result, the system attempts to create the BP again instead of updating it.

You can check whether the key mapping is correct by following the steps in this blog: https://blogs.sap.com/2023/12/01/troubleshooting-business-partner-key-mapping-issues/

If any key mapping entries are missing, you can maintain them using transaction MDG_KM_MAINTAIN and retest the replication.

If you need further assistance, please feel free to create a support case so we can analyze the issue directly in the system.

 

Best regards,

Albert

entry.title: Re: ABAP's Parallel Processing Frameworks: Practical Guide to Performance Tuning of Mass Data Proces
entry.content:
I would challenge the characterization of background jobs as a parallelization framework.

Background processing in ABAP was never designed to provide parallelization services, orchestration, workload partitioning, synchronization, or collision handling. Its primary purpose is to execute fully independent work units in batch mode, detached from interactive processing and without dependencies on other running units of work.

While it is technically possible to start the same report multiple times in parallel, either manually through variants or programmatically via JOB_OPEN, JOB_SUBMIT, and JOB_CLOSE, this does not automatically provide a parallel processing framework. The background job infrastructure simply starts the jobs. It does not offer package creation, workload distribution, coordination between workers, duplicate processing prevention, deadlock avoidance, or result aggregation.

In fact, running identical business logic concurrently can be dangerous if the application was not explicitly designed for parallel execution. Multiple jobs accessing the same business objects or database records may create lock contention, deadlocks, inconsistent processing sequences, or even system-wide bottlenecks.

Therefore, whenever a developer chooses to use background jobs as a mechanism for parallel execution, the entire responsibility for parallelization remains within the application:

  • Work packages must be created and distributed by the calling program.
  • Overlapping packages must be prevented.
  • Enqueue/dequeue strategies must be implemented explicitly.
  • All collision and deadlock scenarios must be considered and handled.
  • Monitoring and orchestration logic must be provided by the application itself.

The background job architecture assumes that the contained program can run independently and safely, regardless of what else is happening in the system. Any parallelization capabilities are therefore built around the job infrastructure, not provided by it. This is a fundamental difference compared to technologies that were specifically designed to support parallel processing and task coordination.

entry.title: Joule Meets Classic UI
entry.content:
Joule meets Classic UI — bringing AI into SAP GUI for Windows and SAP Business Client

Joule meets Classic UI

 SAP Business Client integrates the Joule available in the Fiori Launchpad with native SAP GUI for Windows transactions. When integration is active, an extra window hosts Joule.

Joule can access the current application context and Frontend Actions so it can interact with the open transaction and perform context-specific tasks.

How to use Joule for SAP GUI for Windows transactions
1. Enable the Joule integration via the setting <EnableJouleInSapgui> to true in the <SingleOptions>-segment of the Administrator configuration file, see Administrator Configuration.
2. Define the Edge browser-setting <TrackingPreventionLevel> to Basic or None in the <EdgeSettings>-segment of the Administrator configuration file, see Administrator Configuration.
3. Start SAP Business Client and connect to an SAP Fiori Launchpad connection where Joule has been made available.
4. Open a native SAP GUI for Windows transaction inside SAP Business Client.
5. If a valid Joule instance was located within the Fiori Launchpad environment, a Joule button appears in the top area of the SAP GUI for Windows Header Toolbar. Click the button to launch the Joule UI.
6. Use Joule capabilities mapped to your scenario. Joule can take the current application context and invoke Frontend Actions to perform tasks in the open transaction.

 

Figure 1: Press Joule button

Figure 1: Press Joule button

Figure 2: Joule UI available

Figure 2: Joule UI available

Available Frontend Actions

·     DescribeUI: Access the UI tree of the current SAP GUI screen (useful to locate controls/elements).

·     ExecuteScript: Run an SAP GUI script silently (no user prompts).

·     ExecuteGuidedScript: Run an SAP GUI script with user prompts and UI highlighting.

Important: Frontend Actions must be exposed via Joule capabilities and mapped to the scenarios/use-cases you intend to automate or assist.

Demo-Videos are available.

See also: SAP-samples in github repository

entry.title: Re: Public cloud Finance Ledger setup
entry.content:
Hi Frank,

Short answer: No — you cannot split country-specific (local) accounting principles across two ledgers at company-code level. Public Cloud allows exactly one "local ledger," so your proposed setup (local GAAPs at CoCode level in both 0L and 2L) is what message FINS_ACDOC_CUST499 blocks.

Can I assign a local GAAP to 0L and other local GAAPs on company-code level in 2L?

No. Country-specific accounting principles assigned at company-code level are permitted in only one ledger. The reason is Asset Accounting: only one tax depreciation area can be deployed, and it is derived automatically from the local accounting-principle assignment in G/L. Because a second local ledger would require a second tax depreciation area, the system restricts you to a single local ledger for all company codes. Reference: SAP Note 3530183 and Assign Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes.

Would FINS_ACDOC_CUST499 prohibit my suggested setting?

Yes. That check exists precisely to enforce the one-local-ledger rule ("Only one ledger can have local acctg principles on CoCode level assigned"). If one ledger already carries country-specific principles at company-code level, adding the same type of assignment to a second ledger is rejected.

What is actually supported (recommended pattern):

In the Main Line (0L): assign the group / corporate accounting principle (e.g. IFRS or US GAAP) — typically at ledger level, valid for all company codes.

In the Parallel Line (2L or 3L): make this the single local ledger and assign each company code's local/country GAAP at company-code level here. All local GAAPs, regardless of country, live in this one ledger. Reference: Options for Accounting Principles and Ledgers.

Note 1: 0L technically can be the local ledger (the delivered content even defaults local to 0L), but that still counts as your one local ledger — you cannot then also make 2L local. SAP recommends moving the group principle to 0L and using the second ledger as the local one.

Note 2: the accounting-principle-to-ledger/company-code assignment must be finalized before any postings exist. Changing it afterwards with existing data is not allowed and requires a transaction-data reset. Reference: SAP Note 3530183.

Summary: Route all local/country GAAPs into one ledger (0L or the parallel ledger) at company-code level, and put the group principle on the other ledger. You cannot have country-specific principles at company-code level in two ledgers simultaneously — that is exactly the scenario FINS_ACDOC_CUST499 prevents. Decide and configure this before postings begin.

Hope it helps.

Best regards,
Michal

entry.title: Re: Mail ID Change for the Corresponding Contact With SAP
entry.content:
I understand you would like to change your S-user's email address.

Regarding your request, kindly refer to KBA 3108466 - How to update the email address for S-user IDs - SAP for Me

Abstract below information for your reference:

Resolution

Please contact the company's Super Administrators, User Administrators, or Cloud Administrators (listed here) to change their S-user ID email address for them in the User Management application (guide in KBA 1271545).

S-users can no longer manage their own email addresses in SAP for Me, only the administrators can. Also, administrators cannot change their own email addresses either, so another administrator must do this for them. Product support engineer can not change email for S-user when there are active administrators.

 

Since you are the cloud admin, you won’t be able to edit your own email address. Also, I cannot change the email for an S-user since SAP has policy that when there are active administrators for the company we are not allowed to help update the email address.

Next step:

- Please contact your Super Administrator * to update your email address. 

If you have any difficulties contacting your super admin, please kindly provide your new email address and send this case back to me, I will help you further.

entry.title: Re: IW21 Notification app on the SAP Fiori Launchpad is n...
entry.content:
Hello,

WebGUI (SAP GUI for HTML) apps accessed via Fiori Launchpad tiles have limited and conditional mobile support, whereas native SAP Fiori (SAPUI5-based) apps are designed with responsive, mobile-first principles. While touch-enablement for classical UIs has improved significantly, WebGUI-based apps are not fully supported on all mobile platforms and carry notable limitations compared to native Fiori apps.

Use the FAL to verify whether a specific app is enabled for mobile/tablet — many standard GUI apps default to desktop only, and mobile enablement must be confirmed per app

As you can see in the Fiori apps library for IW21:  https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('IW21')/S32PCE

This is intended to be used only in Desktop(Check the device type field listed).

Thank you.

best regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP Background Job Processing
entry.content:
Thank you for taking the time to create this blog post. I appreciate the effort you put into explaining the classic approach of creating report variants, scheduling jobs through the SAP transaction codes, and monitoring their execution from the backend. For developers, administrators, and anyone learning SAP background processing, understanding these mechanics is valuable and provides important insight into how the underlying technology works.

From my perspective as an Engineering Lead at SAP working in the area of Background Processing Technologies, I would add that this manual approach is most useful for one-time executions, testing, discovery activities, troubleshooting, and educational purposes. Modern applications should generally strive to hide these technical artifacts from business users. The SAP platform provides APIs that allow applications to automate report execution, job scheduling, monitoring, and lifecycle management programmatically. This allows application developers to focus on business functionality rather than exposing the complexity of background processing to end users.

For example, in a mass-processing scenario, a business process owner knows which business data should be processed but typically does not care whether the workload runs synchronously in a dialog work process or asynchronously in a background job. A well-designed application could simply provide a business action such as "Start Mass Data Run" and let the application decide how the workload should be executed based on factors such as data volume, expected runtime, and system conditions. The technical execution model should remain an implementation detail wherever possible.

I would also argue that, in a best-in-class application, users should rarely need to consult transactions such as SM37 during normal business operation. SM37 is primarily an operational and diagnostic tool intended to help administrators and support engineers investigate why a process failed or behaved unexpectedly. If the successful execution of a business process requires users to continuously monitor SM37, inspect logs, and manually resolve technical exceptions, this can be a sign that the architecture of the process could be improved.

The ideal goal is an automated solution that provides business-oriented status information, progress tracking, notifications, and exception handling directly within the application itself. Business users should be able to focus on the outcome of the process, while the application takes responsibility for orchestrating and monitoring the underlying execution technology. Understanding the manual background-job lifecycle remains important, but for modern application design it should increasingly become an implementation detail rather than part of the end-user workflow.

entry.title: Identifying Criteria for Cash Management Users
entry.content:
Hello SAP,

Verily is trying to determine the number of users currently assigned a business category associated with Cash Management (License).

Based on our reporting, Verily has 10 users among these roles.

We also validated against the following based on KB/Joule:

  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAA_APPROVE_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAA_SUBMIT_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAL_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAM2_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAR_MANAGE_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAR_REVIEW_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BFM_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_CASH_OPS_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_CASH_SAC_OPS_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_CONF_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_POA_AUDIT_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_POA_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_SNAPSHOT_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAA_SUBMIT_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAA_APPROVE_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAL_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAM2_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BFM_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_POA_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAM_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_CASH_OPS_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_CONF_PC
  • SAP_FIN_BC_CM_SNAPSHOT_PC 
  •  

No additional user can be identifed. SAP Sales has identified 16 users (6 over our entitlement) for cash management roles per the internal dashboard. 

We also did not find any additional users following: Could SAP support help us identify the criteria that determine the 16 users identified by SAP Sales?

Thank you,

Verily IT


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Identifying Criteria for Cash Management Users
entry.content:
Hi Verily,

 

Regarding this issue, if you wish to manually verify, I suggest the following method:

 

Open the Business Catalogs app.
Enter the Business Catalog ID one by one to check which Business Roles are using these Business Catalogs.
Then, go into the corresponding Business Roles to see which Business Users are assigned to these Business Roles.
For any Business Users assigned to multiple Business Roles, count them only once. This way, you can determine how many users are involved in the Cash Management (License) count.

 

Thanks & Regards,

entry.title: Latest Content for your Product Compliance System
entry.content:
With the latest Product Compliance release, many new compliance requirement versions have been added to the embedded regulatory content. Check out the What's New document on the SAP Help Portal, here.

 Embedded regulatory content, by the numbers:

For more detailed information about compliance requirement versions and other available content, check out the SAP Help Portal pages for:

  • Dangerous Goods Content
  • Product Marketability Content
  • Safety Data Sheet Management Content
  • Safety Data Sheet Authoring Content
  • Occupational Exposure Limits Content  

For Safety Data Sheet Authoring there are 7 new versions and 18 new compliance requirements highlighted by new GHS Classification and Labelling for the USA, Canada and Great Britain:

  • R01878  TW GHS Labeling (Apr 2025)
  • R01877  TW GHS Classification (Apr 2025)
  • R01874  TW Hazardous ingredients (Apr 2025)
  • R01873  TW Components with OELs (Apr 2025)
  • R01872  JP Hazardous ingredients (Dec 2025)
  • R01871  JP Components with OELs (Dec 2025)
  • R01870  US Components with OELs (Jan 2026)

New Compliance Requirements:

  • R01869  US GHS Labeling (Jan 2026)
  • R01868  US GHS Classification (Jan 2026)
  • R01860  US HNOC (Jan 2026)
  • R01859  US Hazards from Change in Physical Form (Jan 2026)
  • R01858  US Hazards associated with chemical reaction products (Jan 2026)
  • R01818  CA GHS Labeling (Jan 2023)
  • R01817  CA GHS Classification (Jan 2023)
  • R01816  GB Exposure Scenario (Mar 2019)
  • R01815  CL Hazardous Ingredients (Feb 2021)
  • R01814  CL Components with OELs (Feb 2021)
  • R01813  US HMIS (Feb 2015)
  • R01812  GB GHS Labeling (Sep 2025)

  • R01809  GB Endocrine Disruptors: Health (Mar 2019)
  • R01808  GB Endocrine Disruptors: Environment (Mar 2019)
  • R01807  FR Medical Surveillance (April 2022)
  • R01806  GB REACH Registration No (Mar 2019)
  • R01805  GB PBT vPvB Assessment (Mar 2019)

For Occupational Exposure Limits there is 1 new compliance requirement and 10 new versions:

New Compliance Requirement

  • R01856 US OEL TSCA Existing Chemical Exposure Limits (Sep 2025)

New Versions

  • R01892 USA. ACGIH Threshold Limit Values (Jan 2026)
  • R01891 Norway. Occupational Exposure Limits (Dec 2025)
  • R01890 Germany. TRGS Occupational Exposure limits (Dec 2025)
  • R01889 Switzerland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jan 2026)
  • R01888 Australia. Occupational Exposure Limits (Nov 2025)

  • R01852 Switzerland. Occupational Exposure Limits (Jul 2025)
  • R01851 Canada. Occupational Exposure Limits - British Columbia (Jun 2025)

For Product Marketability there are a lot of new versions and 1 new compliance requirement: 

  • R01836 AU AIIC Status (Dec 2025)
  • R01837 CA CEPA SNAc Status (Sep 2025)
  • R01838 CA DSL+NDSL Status (Dec 2025)
  • R01879 EU Persistent Organic Pollutants (Dec 2025)
  • R01839 EU Persistent Organic Pollutants (Oct 2025)
  • R01880 EU REACH Annex XVII Status (Oct 2025)
  • R01881 EU REACH SVHC Status (Feb 2026)
  • R01840 EU REACH SVHC Status (Nov 2025)
  • R01882 GT Drug Precursors (Jul 2025)
  • R01849 JP ENCS Status (Oct 2025)
  • R01841 JP ISHA Status (Sep 2025)
  • R01883 JP PDSCL Status (Oct 2025)
  • R01842 KR CCA Accidents List Status (Aug 2025)

For Substance Volume Tracking, we haven't forgotten about SVT, there is new best practice content:

entry.title: Re: 【権限付与依頼】S-IDのメンテナンス通知・インシデント情報閲覧権限について
entry.content:
一. 調査の結果、S ユーザー*が Ariba システムのケースを閲覧できない原因は、Ariba インストール環境のクラウドデータ表示権限（display cloud data on Ariba installation *）が付与されていないことと判明いたしました。

詳細は下記 KBA をご参照ください。
  • 2193754 - S-user is unable to create or view SuccessFactors support cases - SAP for Me
  • 2598170 - What is the Display Cloud Data authorization? - SAP for Me
本件を解決するには、御社の Ariba クラウド管理者へご連絡いただき、KBA「1511008 - How to add or change S-user ID authorizations - SAP for Me」を参照の上、権限の追加・更新を依頼してください。

二. また、同 S ユーザー *がシステムメンテナンス通知を受信できない要因は、Get Notified アプリにて Ariba 関連の通知設定が登録されていないためです。

詳細は下記 KBA をご確認ください。
  • 2896942 - How Super Administrators and Cloud Administrators manage Cloud Notifications for other users in CSNS - SAP for Me
  • 2900069 - How S-users can manage their own Cloud Notifications in the CSNS - SAP for Me
通知設定の解決策は以下 2 通りとなります。
  1. 御社 Ariba クラウド管理者に連絡し、KBA 2896942に従って Get Notified 上の通知設定を代行登録してもらう。
  2. 御社 Ariba クラウド管理者より「Display System Data」または「Edit System Data」権限を付与してもらい、自身で KBA 2900069 の手順に沿って通知設定を実施する。

entry.title: SAP Data Services - Is it possible to hide source table names, target table names and generated SQL
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently evaluating SAP Data Services logging behavior and would appreciate guidance regarding Job Error Log visibility.

Background
----------

A test job was created to evaluate execution and error logging behavior.

Job:
JB_TRACE_LOG_ANALYSIS

Workflow:
WF_TRACE_ANALYSIS

Dataflow:
DF_LOAD_EMPLOYEE

The purpose of the test was to determine whether internal implementation details can be suppressed from the generated logs while still preserving meaningful error information.

Testing Performed
-----------------

The following trace options were disabled during execution:

- Session
- Workflow
- Dataflow

Results
-------

After disabling these trace options, Trace Log verbosity was reduced as expected.

Workflow and Dataflow related execution details were no longer visible in the Trace Log.

However, during a simulated failure (ORA-00001 Unique Constraint Violation), the Job Error Log still displayed technical implementation details, including:

- Source table names
- Target table names
- Generated SQL statements
- Constraint names
- Database object references

Example:

ORA-00001: unique constraint violated

PK_TGT_EMPLOYEE

INSERT INTO TGT_EMPLOYEE ...

FROM SRC_EMPLOYEE

Requirement
-----------

The objective is to retain only high-level error visibility such as:

- Job Failed
- Error Code
- Error Message

while suppressing technical implementation details such as:

- Source table names
- Target table names
- Generated SQL statements
- Constraint names
- Internal object references

Questions
---------

1. Is there any SAP Data Services configuration, repository setting or Management Console option that masks or suppresses source table names, target table names and generated SQL from Job Error Logs?

2. Are Job Error Logs intentionally designed to always expose database-generated SQL exceptions?

3. Is there any SAP-supported approach to display only high-level error information while hiding implementation details?

4. Has anyone implemented a custom logging or error-handling solution to achieve this requirement?

Any guidance, recommendations or SAP Notes references would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: MySQL connectivity from SAP Integration Suite - workaround needed
entry.content:
Hello,

We're trying to set up a connection from SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration), via the JDBC adapter (with Cloud Connector), to an on-premise MySQL database

In the JDBC Data Source configuration, there's no separate type for MySQL, only MariaDB, SQL Server, Oracle, and DB2.

Our research indicates that on-premise MySQL connectivity via the JDBC adapter is not supported.
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003533889

Does a workaround exist for this, or can you give us advice?

Kind regards, Jurgen

entry.content: Thank you for your explanation. I checked the Field List sheet of the Material BOM migration template (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602). The BOM Item worksheet does not contain any document-related columns such as: - Document Type - Document Number - Document Version - Document Part The document fields are only available in the "BOM Item Document Assignment" worksheet (structure S_STPO_DLINK). Therefore, there is currently no place in the BOM Item worksheet where the document key can be maintained for an Item Category D. Could this indicate a limitation or content gap in the Material BOM migration object delivered in release 2602? Thank you.

entry.title: Re: PO Condition Requirement for GR Posting Without Mater...
entry.content:
Standard SAP's purchasing conditions cannot be excluded from material valuation, and conditions configured as statistical will not generate FI postings.

Planned delivery costs do participate in MIRO, indicating that the requirement cannot be met solely through standard MM pricing.

Recommended solutions include using a statistical condition with a BAdI for FI postings during MIGO or implementing custom FI posting logic outside the standard pricing procedure.

Utilizing different currencies for various types of transactions for the same customer may pose compliance challenges, particularly concerning tax reporting and financial statements.

entry.title: S/4HANA Public Cloud Chronicles: (Chapter 1) - Troubleshooting Currency Posting Errors
entry.content:
S/4HANA Public Cloud Chronicles: (Chapter 1) - Troubleshooting Currency Posting Errors

Welcome to 'S/4HANA Public Cloud Chronicles.' This series skips the high-level success stories from a recent S/4HANA Public Cloud go-live. Instead, each chapter provides a technical deep-dive into a specific problem and its exact solution.

In this first chapter of 'S/4HANA Public Cloud Chronicles,' we will dissect a common currency posting error: "No exchange rate found for currency pair XXX/YYY." This post will walk you through the root cause analysis and a simple, practical workaround to resolve this issue without changing your standard rate maintenance procedures.

Project Context and Landscape:

Project Type: Migrating from SAP Private Cloud to SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Organizational Structure:

Problem Statement: 

No exchange rate found for currency pair USD/EUR, exchange rate type M.

Investigation and Root Cause:

We had correctly maintained the exchange rates in the "Manage Currency Exchange Rates " app using an indirect quotation, as was the client's standard practice.

 

The Expectation:

The system should be able to derive the direct rate (USD to EUR) from the above maintained indirect rate for the posting.

The Root Cause:

After further analysis, we discovered the issue was not with the calculation logic, but with a validation check during the posting process. The system was hard-wired to check for the existence of a direct quotation entry (USD to EUR) in the exchange rate table. If it didn't find at least one entry for the direct pair, it would stop with the error, without even attempting to derive the rate from the indirect quotation.

Solution:

The solution was not to change our primary rate maintenance strategy but to implement a simple workaround to satisfy the system's validation check.

We maintained a single, outdated direct exchange rate entry that the system would never actually use for calculations.

Note: Always maintain the conversion direction specified in the error. If the message indicates a EUR to USD rate is missing, maintain the "From" and "To" currencies in that exact order. 

Why This Works:

Since this rate's "Valid From" date is extremely old, the system's transaction logic will never select it for a posting on a current date. However, its mere existence in the table satisfies the initial validation check. The system then proceeds, finds the valid indirect rate for the current date, and correctly performs the currency conversion as intended.

Conclusion:

In summary, this post demonstrated that the "No exchange rate found" error is often caused by a system validation check, not a failure in the conversion logic itself. We have learned that S/4HANA Public Cloud requires the existence of a direct rate entry to proceed, even if it will use an indirect rate for the actual calculation.  

Stay tuned for Chapter 2, where we'll dissect more challenges…

entry.title: Archival of contracts
entry.content:
If you have worked with SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) on high-volume systems, you know how fast database tables can swell. Left unchecked, millions of historical revenue contracts can eventually take a toll on system performance. Fortunately, SAP provides a robust, native archiving framework to handle this. This guide outlines the end-to-end technical procedure for archiving RAR contracts using the archiving object FARR_CONTR. It walks through configuring the mandatory residence time parameters, preparing the information infrastructure, and executing the standard write and deletion programs to safely purge expired contract data from active tables.

  1. Maintain residence time

Residence time refers to the period during which a contract must remain in a specific state before it can be archived.

First check the date of end-usage accounting principle.

In our system, it's 31.07.2024, and today's date is 30.08.2024. Residence time by default is 30 days. Changed that to 28 days.

We can either use

  1. Tcode- SARA

Give archiving object FARR_CONTR. Click on customizing. Maintain residence time

     2. SPRO--> Financial accounting--> Revenue Accounting--> Revenue Accounting contracts-->Archiving-->Define residence time

2. Maintain Infostructure

  1. Use SARA/SARI

SAP_FARR_CONTR is the default structure by SAP. We can create our own too.

Create and activate

2. Using SPRO

SPRO--> Financial accounting--> Revenue Accounting--> Revenue Accounting contracts-->Archiving--> Maintain infostructure

3. Run program

FARR_CONTR_WRI

We can run in test mode first. Selection date it picks according to the residence time

If the program is successful, you will get such output:

4. Run deletion program

FARR_CONTR_DEL/RFARR_CONTR_AR02

If the run is successful, it will give output:

And then we can check the related tables, data will be deleted

You can also view the log using SARA

Conclusion

Following this structured archiving process ensures that old contract data is safely offloaded without interrupting daily revenue accounting operations. By adjusting the residence time and validating data across writing and deletion phases, you maintain a lean database while keeping historical records accessible via the archive information system (SARI). Regularly monitoring these runs through transaction (SARA) is highly recommended to maintain data integrity and optimize system performance over the long term.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: Smarter, Faster, More Connected Integrations - Q2'2026 News
entry.content:
Welcome to the latest product highlights for SAP Integration Suite. In this update, we’re sharing newly released capabilities designed to help you accelerate integration development, simplify operations, and connect SAP and third-party landscapes with greater confidence. From AI-assisted API and error-resolution features to new adapters, Edge Integration Cell enhancements, event-driven monitoring, and expanded data center availability, updates in Q2 2026 help integration teams build faster, run more reliably, and deliver more value across the enterprise. So, let’s dig into it!

Integration for AI

The introduction of API-Centric Integration and MCP Gateway in SAP Integration Suite bring API management, integration mediation, and agent readiness into one harmonized integration layer that enables customers to build, govern, and scale the next generation of enterprise integration scenarios, including AI-powered use cases.

With API-centric integration, practitioners can design APIs as first-class integration artifacts that combine routing, transformation, enrichment, logging, security policies, rate limiting, and lifecycle governance in one consistent authoring and runtime experience, rather than treating API proxies and integration flows as separate implementation layers. Building on this foundation, the MCP Gateway aggregates SAP APIs, non-SAP APIs, integration flows, data sources, and external MCP servers as governed, discoverable MCP tools for AI agents, while enforcing enterprise controls such as authentication, authorization, rate limiting, payload protection, monitoring, and traceability.

Together, these capabilities help customers turn existing business processes and APIs into secure, reusable, AI-ready interfaces that can be consumed by both traditional applications and agentic AI scenarios without sacrificing governance, operational visibility, or control. Find resources about these exciting updates: One Blog, Two Strategic Topics: API-Centric Integration and MCP Gateway

AI for Integration

These new AI-powered capabilities in SAP Integration Suite help you work faster across design, documentation, testing, and operations:

AI-assisted message processing error resolution

Restore integrations faster and operate with greater confidence by using embedded AI to identify where an error occurred, explain its likely root cause, highlight the first failed step, and recommend a resolution directly within SAP Integration Suite monitoring. By making analysis easy to copy, save, and share, the feature improves collaboration between integration teams, supports faster pattern recognition across recurring issues, reduces mean time to recovery, and lowers the learning curve for new developers, ultimately helping customers keep business-critical processes running more reliably while using AI capabilities in a guided and economical way. Documentation: AI-assisted Error Resolution

 AI-assisted API specification and documentation generation

This feature helps you accelerate API onboarding by using generative AI to derive OpenAPI specifications and documentation from back-end service definitions. Instead of manually modelling endpoints, request and response structures, parameters, and descriptions, teams can generate a standards-aligned API contract and supporting documentation as a starting point, then review and refine it before publishing. This reduces repetitive specification work, improves consistency across APIs, and helps developers expose back-end services faster with documentation that is easier to understand, test, and adopt. Documentation: Generate OpenAPI Specification with AI Assistance.

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

The API mocking capability in SAP Business Accelerator Hub help you test and validate APIs earlier by generating mock responses directly from OpenAPI specifications, including support for multiple response types, status codes, and example payloads. Developers can use an AI-generated API sandbox as part of the API try-out experience to execute operations such as GET, PUT, POST, and DELETE against mock endpoints using sample or custom request data, without depending on a live back-end implementation. This accelerates API evaluation, improves contract-first development and consumer onboarding, and gives customers a faster, lower-friction way to explore, test, and adopt APIs before production connectivity is available. Documentation: Trying Out APIs in a AI-generated API Sandbox.

Edge Integration Cell: Next-generation hybrid integration runtime

These Edge Integration Cell updates make the hybrid runtime more configurable, observable, and deployment-ready for distributed integration scenarios:

▪️ Support for the partner directory: Edge Integration Cell now supports use of the partner directory, enabling integration flows running on the edge runtime to read partner-specific configuration data at runtime. This makes it possible to externalize and dynamically resolve partner-related parameters instead of hard-coding them into integration logic, which simplifies the operation of multi-partner scenarios and reduces the effort required to maintain partner-specific variants. The value for customers is greater flexibility and scalability when running hybrid integrations across distributed partner landscapes. 

▪️ Custom system monitoring solution: Customers can now connect an external Prometheus instance to Edge Integration Cell using SAP-documented exporter endpoints and exposed monitoring data. Practitioners can configure Prometheus to scrape SAP metrics and then build dashboards and alerts in their existing observability tooling, with deployment options depending on TLS encryption and built-in monitoring requirements. This gives operations teams more control over how edge runtimes are monitored, supports integration into established monitoring landscapes, and helps customers detect issues earlier without relying only on default monitoring options.

▪️ Runtime activation on SAP Cloud Infrastructure: Edge Integration Cell runtime activation for SAP Integration Suite on SAP Cloud Infrastructure is now available on demand for supported regions, enabling hybrid integration scenarios between SAP Cloud Infrastructure-hosted SAP Integration Suite tenants and Edge Integration Cell runtime installations. This allows customers to place integration processing closer to regional workloads, helping reduce latency and support data residency or compliance requirements through sovereignty-aligned deployments. For integration teams, the update expands deployment choice and makes edge-based runtime adoption more practical for customers operating in regulated or region-sensitive environments.

Event-Driven Architecture

Support for health monitoring of SAP landscapes and advanced event mesh brokers through SAP Cloud ALM enables practitioners to monitor broker health and key SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh metrics from a central SAP Cloud ALM dashboard. By exposing broker metrics directly in the monitoring entry point used for SAP landscapes, teams can more easily observe event-driven integration infrastructure, identify potential issues earlier, and align event mesh operations with existing SAP landscape monitoring processes. This gives customers better operational visibility, faster issue detection, and a more consistent way to manage the reliability of event-driven integration scenarios. Documentation: Access SAP Integration Suite, Advanced Event Mesh Metrics on SAP Cloud ALM Dashboard

Connectivity & Adapters: pre-built content for faster time to value

The following adapters and integration content were released in Q2 2026 to help customers connect SAP and third-party applications faster with prebuilt, standardized connectivity:

  • Adobe Sign Adapter
  • Google Workspace Adapter 
  • JDBC Receiver Adapter
  • Twilio Adapter

New Data Center Availability

  • Europe (Frankfurt) through Microsoft Azure.
  • Japan (Tokyo) through Google Cloud.
  • United States (Sterling) on SAP-hosted infrastructure

More details on SAP Discovery Center Service - SAP Integration Suite.

--

➡️To see some of these updates in action, join our monthly update webinar on July 28th. Every last Tuesday of the month you have the chance to meet product experts and get a deep dive into the latest features for SAP Integration Suite - don't miss it!

entry.title: Re: Repeated RAP update of business partner is causing error
entry.content: DATA is_input TYPE ZMMCLASCO1920001=>ty_update. DATA lv_error TYPE c LENGTH 1. DATA lv_message TYPE c LENGTH 200. CONSTANTS lc_tax1 TYPE c LENGTH 4 VALUE 'tax1'. CONSTANTS lc_tax2 TYPE c LENGTH 4 VALUE 'tax2'. DATA lt_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE i_businesspartnertp_3\_businesspartnertaxnumber. lt_create = VALUE #( ( businesspartner = is_input-businesspartner %target = VALUE #( ( %cid = lc_tax1 businesspartner = is_input-businesspartner bptaxtype = 'FR1' %control-bptaxtype = if_abap_behv=>mk-on bptaxnumber = COND #( WHEN is_input-siretnew IS NOT INITIAL AND is_input-siretold IS INITIAL THEN is_input-siretnew ) %control-bptaxnumber = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ( %cid = lc_tax2 businesspartner = is_input-businesspartner bptaxtype = 'FR2' %control-bptaxtype = if_abap_behv=>mk-on bptaxnumber = COND #( WHEN is_input-sirennew IS NOT INITIAL AND is_input-sirenold IS INITIAL THEN is_input-sirennew ) %control-bptaxnumber = if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) ) ) ). READ TABLE lt_create ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_line>) INDEX 1. IF sy-subrc EQ 0. DELETE <ls_line>-%target WHERE bptaxnumber IS INITIAL. ENDIF. IF <ls_line>-%target IS NOT INITIAL. CLEAR: lv_error, lv_message. DATA(lv_lines) = lines( <ls_line>-%target ). MODIFY ENTITIES OF i_businesspartnertp_3 ENTITY businesspartner CREATE BY \_businesspartnertaxnumber SET FIELDS WITH lt_create MAPPED DATA(ls_mapped) REPORTED DATA(ls_reported) FAILED DATA(ls_failed). COMMIT ENTITIES BEGIN RESPONSE OF i_businesspartnertp_3 FAILED DATA(ls_failed_commit) REPORTED DATA(ls_reported_commit). COMMIT ENTITIES END. ENDIF.

entry.title: You may have outgrown your ERP systems - What can you do?
entry.content:
Central Finance supports several key business scenarios that help organizations simplify and modernize their finance operations.

First, it enables process centralization and shared services by bringing finance activities together into a single operating model. This reduces duplication, creates economies of scale, lowers operating costs, and standardizes processes across the organization.  Many organizations experiencing growth will have a difficult time to manage financial operations with old technology and old processes.

In addition organizations who grow quickly as part of M&A processes may also struggle with using old technology.  Central Finance allows newly acquired or reorganized business units to be integrated into a centralized financial environment without requiring an immediate ERP replacement. This enables faster integration while minimizing disruption to ongoing operations.

Another important use case for central finance is integrated planning, financial consolidation, transaction processing, and reporting. By providing a single, harmonized financial data foundation, organizations can improve budgeting, forecasting, period-end close, intercompany reconciliation, and enterprise-wide financial reporting.  In addition, AI can be built on a firm foundation ensuring accuracy of the data being reported internally and externally.

Central Finance also supports centralized finance operations by enabling key processes such as General Ledger, Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and Asset Accounting to be managed consistently across multiple business units or legal entities.

For global financial reporting, Central Finance delivers a single source of truth. Financial information is available at the line-item level across all connected systems, based on a common global finance template. This provides consistent reporting, greater transparency, and improved auditability across the enterprise.

Finally, Central Finance promotes data harmonization, validation, and standardization. By aligning master data, standardizing financial structures, and applying centralized validation and review processes, organizations improve data quality, strengthen governance, and ensure consistent, reliable financial information for reporting and decision-making.

Overall, Central Finance enables organizations to streamline finance operations, accelerate transformation initiatives, improve reporting consistency, and gain real-time visibility into financial performance across the enterprise while providing a platform for AI and other innovation initiatives.

Discover how large organizations can use Central Finance to bring out their best by driving efficiency, reducing manual tasks and improving standardization across their landscape.

For more information, click on the link below:
https://www.sap.com/products/financial-management/central-finance.html

Additional information can be found here:
https://www.sap.com/products/financial-management/central-finance.html
https://events.sap.com/eur-centralfinance-and-beyond-2026/en_us/home.html
https://events.sap.com/us-sap-central-finance-and-beyond-conference-2026/en_us/home.html

entry.title: BW Modenization continued with the SAP BW Query Template Generator
entry.content:
Motivation

With SAP Business Data Cloud and all it's innovations towards the Autonomous Enterprise there are and will be a lot of new capabilities available for customers to increase insights into their data and maximize efficency of business processes and workflows with AI-driven agents and Intelligent Content (formerly Intelligent Applications).

To achieve this a robust and consistent data foundation is important in terms of data quality and consistency of metadata throughout all business applications and LOB solutions offered by SAP. At the same time non-SAP data and 3rd party sources must be integrated seamlessly. The basis for this Business Data Fabric is Business Data Cloud. The core and most granular artefacts of a Business Data Fabric are Data Products. Each Data Product is well defined and unique throughout the landscape and are combined to more complex sophisticated models and Analytic Views to fuel the Intelligent Content built on top to expose the data in an easy consumable way .

Looking at customers of SAP Business Warehouse or SAP BW/4HANA they already invested a lot of effort  to harmonize and consolidate the data located in their source systems to expose the data in the most meaningful way to their end users. There already is a huge value in such existing, historical data. Hence, SAP delivers tools which support customers to be able to efficiently leverage such data instantly with the new technologies and at the same time support a smooth transition of their existing landscape to the new platforms and technologies.

SAP already introduced the 'Data Product Generator' for SAP BW 7.5 and SAP BW/4HANA which is described in the first blog: BW Modernisation with the SAP BW Data Product Generator.

In this blog a second tool is introduced: the 'Query Template Generator' which supports SAP BW / SAP BW/4HANA customers to transition their existing BW Queries to SAP BDC and more specifically to SAP Datasphere.

The difference of these tools is, that the Data Product Generator exposes existing InfoProviders of SAP BW to local delta tables in the object store of SAP Datasphere. From there they can instantly be used in further developments or secondly Data Products can be generated out of such tables using the Data Sharing Cockpit of SAP Datasphere (or the Data Product Studio when available). Doing so such customer defined Data Products can instantly be exposed to e.g. Databricks to be consumed in AI/ML tools.

On the other side the 'Query Template Generator' is targeted to the migration of BW Queries to native SAP Datasphere artifacts to bring the OLAP logic of a BW Query to SAP Datasphere and finally SAP BDC.

In late 2026 there is a third tool planned to be delivered the 'BW Migration Assistant' which supports SAP BW customers to migrate their ETL processes and the logic built within to native SAP Datasphere ETL processes.

Prerequisites

Update your Eclipse based Modeling Tools to the latest version (minimum 2026-06)

Please also refer to SAP Note 3590400 for further information.

 

Walkthrough the Query Template Generator

Let's have a look at the Query Template Generator which is only available in the BW Modelling Tools in Eclipse. To access the UI of the Query Template Generator in Eclipse it must be added as a new view in the main Eclipse UI.

The main view where every Query Template creation starts is quite straight forward:

At first the right 'BW Project' needs to be selected to specify the context which connected BW contains the source objects (BEx Queries) to be migrated. Secondly a list with all protocols of former Query Template generation runs appears with the corresponding status information. A given run can be copied (including the related customizing) to take it as a basis for another migration run.

To start a new Query Template the highlighted button at the top right menu bar (last screenshot) must be selectes and a small wizard will appear to enter the necessary parameters. In the first step the BW Query must be selected which should be migrated to SAP Datasphere artifacts:

After having selected the BW Query a description for the Query Template can be entered. The field 'Description' does not represent the Description of the Query.

In the next step of the process the Target SAP Datasphere System must be selected and a Space as a target where all generated artifacts will be generated. In addition a 'prefix' can be (optionally) specified which will be generated as a leading part of the technical name of the migrated Analytic Model:

After confirming the customizig with the 'Finish' Button, the Query Template is finalized and the general information of the settings made are displayed:

Next to the Tab displaying the general settings there is a second interesting Tab 'Mappings' where you can see all dependent Objects of the BW Query selected and to which type to SAP Datasphere each BW object is migrated including the 'Semantic Usage':

Since the Query Template Generator resolved the query downwards to the InfoProvider and the InfoObjects fields, there can be quite a lot of objects to be created in SAP Datasphere depending on the complexity of the Query and the underlying data model! To start the migration process the highlighted button in the next screenshot must be pressed:

If this button is pressed, there will be another PopUp asking for a final confirmation to start the migration.

In the example BW Query taken in this Blog, the Query was built upon a CompositeProvider which contains only one advanced DataStore Object consisting of fields. Only one column of this ADSO is an InfoObject. The ADSO has 39 columns (for each of the column a dimension or keyfigure is created consisting of a view and a local table in SAP Datasphere. 

The only InfoObject contains a hierarchy table which is also reflected in the migration process, thus for this dimension a 'hierarchy with directory' and all related metadata views) are created.

For the BW Query an Analytic Model is created as the central object for further Analytics on top of it.

After the migration process was completed as a result of this BW Query and the underlying model 81 native SAP Datasphere artifacts had been created in the Space specified in the Query Template.

 

Summary

The Query Template Generator supports customers of SAP Business Warehouse in the optimize, modernize and innovate journey to bring their existing SAP BW systems to SAP Business Data Cloud. It supports specifically in the modernize Phase because the tool only migrated the Metadata of the Query to native SAP Datasphere Objects. It is the decision of the customers to decide about which source object will be connected to provide the data. Either a Data Product (generated with the BW Data Product Generator) can be taken (data is still coming from SAP BW) or the customer can innovate his existing scenario by connecting a SAP managed Data Product which might deliver the same data - but directly from an SAP Business application.

entry.title: How to transport COPA characteristic descriptions and Values from Dev to prod
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

How to transport chaanges of COPA characteristic descriptions (KEA5) and Values (KES1) from development system to production system.

Could you pleaes provide step by step process. Our current system SAP S/4 Hana 2023 on Premise. S/4 Hana 1909 was implemented in 2022. later in 2025 it was upgraded to S/4 Hana 2023.

Could you please provide what options I have to select while generating transport request KE3I. 
This TR movement should not impact COPA derivation strategy steps (KEDR)

 

Thanks,

Srinu Mummidi

entry.title: What’s new in Artificial Intelligence in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2608
entry.content:
AI is rapidly evolving from a productivity enhancer into a true business collaborator. With the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release, SAP takes another significant step toward its vision of the Autonomous Enterprise, where AI is no longer a separate tool but an integral part of everyday business processes. New innovations across agentic AI, Joule, and embedded AI capabilities help organizations automate routine work, uncover insights faster, and streamline decision-making—while keeping people firmly in control.

In this blog, we'll explore the most important AI innovations delivered with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. These enhancements are relevant for a wide range of roles, empowering teams in finance, supply chain, professional services, and beyond to make faster, more informed decisions and focus on strategic business outcomes.

 

Joule Work

In this release, we are thrilled to introduce Joule Work, a dynamic workspace that fundamentally reimagines how work gets done across your enterprise. Going far beyond a simple chat, Joule Work acts as your central engagement layer, intelligently interpreting your intent to surface the right insights, automate routine work, and coordinate powerful AI agents across all your systems. By unifying conversations and AI-generated spaces for complex problem-solving, it transforms siloed tasks into a single, seamless experience. This allows your teams to move away from transactional processes and focus on what truly matters: making the critical decisions that move the business forward.

 

Conversations

Conversations in Joule Work are designed to facilitate natural language interactions, enabling users to quickly ask questions and receive insights directly within the workspace. This functionality makes accessing data and obtaining immediate answers effortless, breaking down communication barriers and accelerating decision-making processes. Conversations integrate seamlessly with your existing systems, allowing for real-time interactions, making sure users have the information they need at their fingertips without jumping between multiple applications.

 

Spaces

Spaces are dynamic, AI-generated environments within Joule Work that adapt to facilitate complex problem-solving and collaborative tasks. Unlike static dashboards or document sharing platforms, Spaces intelligently structure resources and tools according to the needs of the task at hand. Spaces provide an interactive canvas where information, analytics, and dialogue converge. This flexibility supports diverse business processes, encouraging innovation, efficiency, and teamwork across departments.

 

Develop

The Develop capability, facilitated through Joule Studio, empowers organizations to create and deploy custom agents and automations tailored to their specific business processes. This feature puts the power of AI in your hands, allowing both developers and business users to design specialized workflows that seamlessly integrate with existing systems. Develop enables businesses to extend the functionality of Joule Work beyond out-of-the-box solutions, accommodating unique requirements and enhancing scalability. With intuitive tools and support for advanced scripting, users can craft intelligent automations that optimize operations and drive productivity.

 

SAP-managed Joule

The SAP-managed approach for Joule is our new, recommended method for activating and operating your generative AI copilot. It's designed to dramatically simplify your experience and accelerate time-to-value by replacing the complex, manual setup with a streamlined, self-service activation process directly within the SAP for Me portal. With SAP handling the technical management and integration, your team is freed from significant IT overhead. This new model also ensures you are best positioned to receive the latest Joule innovations, including new agents and capabilities like Joule Work, as soon as they become available, often with minimal to no additional configuration required on your part.

 

Autonomous Finance

Financial Close Assistant (release 2608.03)

Financial close demands tight coordination across clearing, reconciliation, journal entries, and analysis—all of this under deadline pressure. The Financial Closing Assistant empowers the Financial Close Accountant to orchestrate an intelligent agent-based team that handles these tasks autonomously within set boundaries. From receivables clearing to intercompany reconciliation and key performance indicator analysis, agents execute critical close activities with transparency and audit-ready precision, enabling a faster, more reliable close.

 

Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent (release 2608.02)

Finance teams can now streamline accounts receivable and payable clearing with the new Receivables and Payables Clearing Agent, an AI-powered capability that analyzes open items and generates balanced clearing proposals for accountant review. Integrated with dedicated SAP Fiori apps, the agent intelligently matches invoices and open items using commonly used reference fields and provides clear explanations for its recommendations.

By accelerating clearing activities, reducing manual matching effort, and minimizing human error, the solution helps improve operational efficiency while giving accountants greater confidence in the review process. Approved proposals can be automatically posted as clearing documents, enabling a faster and more streamlined financial close process.

 

Journal Entry Agent (release 2608.03)

Creating journal entries from spreadsheet data is now faster and more efficient with the Journal Entry Agent. This AI-powered capability analyzes uploaded spreadsheet files submitted through Joule and automatically generates accurate, compliant journal entry proposals. Users can review, adjust, and post these proposals directly in the Manage Journal Entry Proposals app, significantly reducing manual effort in journal entry processing.

By automatically mapping spreadsheet columns to the required journal entry fields, the agent helps improve accuracy, increase transparency through access to source files, and streamline financial workflows. Users are notified when proposals are ready for review and can manage the complete lifecycle—from review and adjustment to posting—within a single application. The result is a more efficient journal entry process that supports faster period-end closing while maintaining transparency and control.

 

International Trade Classification Agent

Classifying products for international trade can be a complex and time-consuming process, requiring deep knowledge of customs regulations and commodity code structures. The International Trade Classification Agent helps simplify this task by using AI to analyze product characteristics and automatically recommend appropriate commodity codes and customs tariff numbers for international shipping.

Designed to support trade classification specialists, the agent provides transparent, explainable recommendations based on product information and applicable trade regulations. Each proposed classification is accompanied by a clear rationale, making it easier for users to review, validate, and approve results. By automating classification activities and reducing reliance on manual research, the solution helps organizations save time, improve compliance, and minimize the risk of errors through a robust and auditable decision-making process.

 

E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent (release 2608.02)

As electronic invoicing mandates continue to expand globally, organizations face increasing pressure to resolve invoice submission errors quickly to avoid disruptions in order-to-cash processes. The E-Invoicing Submission Error Resolution Agent helps simplify and accelerate error handling by providing contextual insights, recommended resolution actions, and guided root cause analysis directly within the workflow.

The agent analyzes and classifies technical error messages returned by tax authorities and submission networks, distinguishing between technical issues—such as connectivity, infrastructure, or data-format problems—and business-impacting issues that require changes to document content, master data, or financial postings. By delivering standardized guidance, clear explanations, and documented reasoning for each proposed resolution, the solution promotes consistent error handling, improves auditability, and helps organizations meet mandatory submission deadlines while reducing the risk of non-compliance penalties.

 

Statutory Report Analysis Agent (release 2608.02)

Reviewing statutory and VAT reports can be a time-consuming process, particularly for organizations managing large volumes of tax declarations across multiple reporting periods. The Statutory Report Analysis Agent helps tax and finance teams reduce this effort by automatically analyzing reporting trends, identifying anomalies, and performing root cause analysis to uncover potential compliance risks before reports are submitted.

By comparing current reporting results with historical periods, the agent detects fluctuations, unusual transactions, and outliers that may require attention. It analyzes contributing factors across dimensions such as tax codes, document types, and business partners, and provides consolidated summaries of key figures, identified issues, and root cause findings. In addition, the agent delivers actionable recommendations to help users validate variances, investigate irregularities, and make informed decisions with confidence. The result is a more efficient and data-driven review process that improves compliance readiness without increasing manual effort.

 

Autonomous Supply Chain

Production Master Data Readiness Agent (release 2608.02)

The Product Master Data Readiness Agent assists Master Data Specialists in efficiently creating and updating manufacturing master data, ensuring faster and more accurate preparation for production. The agent enables quick production initiation with minimal manual effort when setting up new materials, prevents production interruptions, and supports consistent planning through the application of proven patterns.

It proactively addresses master data quality issues to avoid unexpected problems during production. For existing product masters with incomplete or potentially incorrect data, the agent suggests values for MRP-related fields using SAP-RPT-1 and similar materials' context.

Additionally, it examines available routings and suggests reusable or copyable options using a machine learning algorithm to identify similar BOMs and their routing through production version tracing

 

Production Planning and Operation agent

The Production Planning and Operations Agent enables users to release production orders through natural language in Joule. It allows for a response to automated checks concerning material, capacity, and scheduling availability by selecting suggested alternative components and adjusting schedules. Users can approve these changes to authorize Joule to release the orders.

 

Inventory Supervisor agent (release 2608.02)

Keeping inventory under control requires continuous visibility into stock performance and the ability to quickly respond to emerging issues. The Inventory Supervisor Agent helps inventory managers turn inventory KPIs into corrective action by providing end-to-end transparency into stock situations, proactively identifying inventory risks, and recommending optimization measures.

The agent continuously monitors inventory performance and performs real-time and ad-hoc stock analysis to detect situations that require attention, such as slow-moving or obsolete inventory. It goes beyond issue detection by analyzing root causes, assessing business impact in terms of quantity and value, and providing actionable recommendations to improve inventory health. By automating analysis and delivering guided insights, the solution reduces manual effort, enables faster decision-making, and helps organizations proactively address inventory challenges before they impact operations.

 

Professional Services

Smart Suggestions for Time Entry (release 2608)

With release 2608, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces Smart Suggestions for Time Entry, an AI-powered capability that automatically proposes timesheet entries based on an employee’s recent booking history and recurring work patterns. Instead of manually searching for projects, tasks, and cost objects, employees can review and confirm pre-populated suggestions with a single click, significantly reducing administrative effort and improving user experience. By streamlining time recording, the feature helps increase on-time submissions, reduce booking errors, and provide more accurate project cost data for project managers and finance teams. As the system learns from individual booking behavior, suggestions become increasingly relevant, enabling faster, more reliable, and effortless time tracking.

 

Environment, Health, and Safety

AI-assisted compliance management (release 2608)

With the 2608 release, SAP S/4HANA Cloud introduces AI-assisted compliance management for Environment, Health, and Safety (EHS), helping organizations accelerate the setup of compliance obligations from regulatory and permit documents. AI automatically extracts and structures relevant information from PDF source documents, identifies compliance requirements, and proposes corresponding tasks such as maintenance activities, notifications, and reporting actions. A side-by-side document viewer provides full traceability between source content and generated obligations, improving audit readiness while reducing manual effort, data-entry errors, and the risk of overlooking compliance-relevant requirements. This innovation enables faster, more accurate, and scalable compliance management across complex regulatory landscapes.

 

Joule for Consultants

Custom Knowledge Grounding

Organizations can now enhance SAP Joule for Consultants with Custom Knowledge Grounding, enabling administrators to connect company-specific documents and knowledge sources through SAP AI Core. By combining organizational content with SAP-curated expertise, Joule delivers more relevant, context-aware, and tailored responses. This helps consultants spend less time searching across multiple sources and make faster, better-informed decisions that align with their organization's standards and best practices.

 

Expert Workspace

The new Expert Workspace provides a powerful way to organize and manage AI Experts—specialized AI assistants configured with custom instructions and optional reference materials for more focused, context-rich interactions. Users can now create up to 25 Experts, supporting a wider range of projects, workflows, and areas of expertise.

To improve transparency and management, Expert Workspace now displays the user's total attachment storage allocation of 1 GB across all Experts, while maintaining a 10 MB per file upload limit. Enhanced search, tagging, and filtering capabilities make it easier to locate and organize Experts, ensuring quick access even as expert libraries grow.

Centralized Expert Library for Organizations

Administrators can now establish a Centralized Expert Library, allowing organizations to curate, manage, and publish reusable Experts for all users. Through a streamlined workflow, organizations can capture expert knowledge from experienced team members and make it available across the business, promoting consistency, knowledge sharing, and faster onboarding. The library remains private to the customer organization, while administrators retain full control over publishing, updating, and retiring shared Experts.

UI and Performance Enhancements

SAP Joule for Consultants continues to evolve with several usability and productivity improvements designed to create a more seamless user experience.

A refreshed welcome screen provides a modernized entry point and easier access to key capabilities. Users can now maintain up to 50 active conversations, a significant increase from the previous limit of 10, enabling more effective management of parallel projects and workstreams.

The introduction of a Retry capability allows users to regenerate responses without restarting the conversation. Whether the goal is to create a longer explanation, a more concise summary, or incorporate additional instructions, answers can be refined with just a few clicks. In addition, users can now edit their original prompts, making it easier to add context, clarify requirements, and improve response quality without beginning a new conversation. Together, these enhancements make interactions with Joule more flexible, efficient, and collaborative.

 

The Future is Intelligent
The 2608 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition demonstrates our commitment to building an ERP that is not just a system of record, but a true partner in your success. By embedding AI across the suite, we are helping you unlock new levels of productivity, enhance decision-making, and create a more intuitive and enjoyable user experience for everyone.

This is just the beginning, and I can't wait to see how you leverage these new capabilities to drive your business forward.

 

🎦Register for our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 Early Release Series! 

Between July 13-17, we are hosting a series of 24 expert-led live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608 release. On our landing page you can register for the entire series or pick the sessions most relevant to you. 

🔎Find all our assets  

The release 2608 showcases more than 130 innovation highlights across the portfolio. Check out our release document for all the details!

We at Cloud ERP and SCM Product Success offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Click on the enablement wheel below and check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you:

entry.title: Re: How does IBT1 field Direction work?

entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

We are unable to consume the SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model OData service in SAP Business Application Studio to create a Fiori application. In the SAP Fiori application generator, selecting Connect to a System and the destination PAPM_Cloud_UM_INSTANCE returns HTTP 400: “The server returned an error. Bad request.

below are the destination details for the PaPM UM

Name: PAPM UM Name=PAPM_Cloud_UM_INSTANCE
Description=Universal Model destination for cloud app
ProxyType=Internet
Type=HTTP
URL=https://****.eu10.papm.cloud.sap/ace/ext
Authentication=OAuth2ClientCredentials
clientId=sb-papm-cloud-api!b405831|sap-papm-cloud!b79598
clientSecret=<removed>
tokenServiceURL=https://****.authentication.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/oauth/token
tokenServiceURLType=Dedicated

Additional Properties
HTML5.DynamicDestination=true
HTML5.ForwardAuthToken=false
HTML5.Timeout=60000
WebIDEAdditionalData=full_url
WebIDEEnabled=true
WebIDEUsage=odata_gen

We also tried Connect to an OData Service using the PaPM UM service URL:

with the service username and password from the service key, but the generator returned:

Authentication failed: Unable to access any service catalogs

Could you please help us identify the root cause and the correct configuration for consuming  PaPM UM OData service into BAS?

Thanks and regards,
Rajesh
